utterances.text: Markus, thanks for the time. You mentioned you are running a head-to-head between Cadenza and Salesloft. I want to make today useful for that comparison.
Yes, we're at the final round. Salesloft just gave us their best-and-final yesterday. We are eighteen reps today and growing to thirty next year, and we need to pick a partner this month.
Three things will decide this for us. First, deliverability. We are a small energy company and our domain has limited reputation. Cold reply rates matter to us a lot.
On deliverability, both platforms give you the basics. Where we differentiate is the warmup tooling and the proactive DKIM and DMARC alignment checks built into the admin console.
Speaking of which, my team is in the middle of switching DKIM keys this week. Anything we should be careful about?

Yes, definitely. When you tighten DMARC to p equal reject without aligning the sending platform's DKIM, you'll see your cold reply rate fall off a cliff. Talk to our deliverability team before you push the change.
Good warning. Second decision factor: AI workflow. Salesloft's AI feels like a sidecar — generate a few subject lines, summarize a call. What's different on your side?

Our AI is embedded in the workflow, not bolted on. It drafts the next step, reorders sequences based on signals, and learns from the rep's own writing voice over time. For your team size that compounds quickly.
Can I see that live in a demo before we sign? I want my reps to evaluate it, not just me.
Yes. I can set up a hands-on session with three of your reps next week. Two-hour working session with real prospect data, no scripted demo.
Third decision factor is price. Salesloft is twelve percent below your initial quote per seat. Can you match that, or do I need to make a value argument internally?
I would rather make the value argument together. If your team books one additional meeting per week per rep thanks to the AI Workflow advantage, the cost difference disappears in two months.
Fair. I'll need the case study with that math to take to my CFO. Send it together with the workshop slots and we will pick a path next week.
Will do. Reply by end of day with the case study, three workshop slots, and a deliverability checklist for your DKIM change.
